title: Mellow Roasted Spanish Baby Lettuce Cocido Madrileno
description: A hearty and comforting Spanish dish featuring roasted baby lettuce leaves, served with a side of fried rice and crunchy breadcrumbs, perfect for a lazy Sunday afternoon. This kosher-friendly recipe is easy to make and packed with flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the baby lettuce leaves with olive oil, garlic, kosher salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the lettuce leaves out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 15 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
While the lettuce is roasting, heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the cooked fry grind rice, toasted khao khuas, and breadcrumbs.
Cook the rice mixture for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until it is crispy and golden brown.
To serve, divide the roasted lettuce leaves among four plates and top each plate with a scoop of the fried rice mixture.
Garnish with additional toasted breadcrumbs and chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
Let the dish cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
